title: Stisseria concolor
taxonomy: Stisseria concolor is classified within the Asclepiadaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as milkweeds and dogbanes. However, the Asclepiadaceae family has been reclassified as part of the Apocynaceae family. The genus Stisseria is a part of the Apocynaceae family and is native to South Africa. This plant is an evergreen climber with small, greenish-white flowers. The specific classification of Stisseria concolor may vary depending on the source or taxonomic perspective.
